perturbation.original_text: Distributor shall pay Company within thirty (30) days of Distributor's or, as the case may be, the end-user's receipt of Products.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor shall remit payment to Company within sixty (60) days of Distributor's or, as the case may be, the end-user's receipt of Products.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'pay' has been changed to 'remit payment' with a change in the timeline. This introduces a contradiction as 'pay' typically implies immediate or shorter-term settlement, while 'remit payment' may imply a different or extended process. The change from thirty (30) days to sixty (60) days creates a direct contradiction in the payment terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement upon 30 days prior written notice to the other upon the occurrence of any of the following events: (A) the Distributor's failure to make full and prompt payment to the Company of all sums due and owing to it; (b) either party's default in the performance of any of the material, terms, conditions, obligations, undertakings, covenants or liabilities set forth herein and such default is not cured within a commercially reasonable time after the defaulting party has been notified of the default by the other party and (c) as otherwise expressly provided herein.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'thirty (30) days' to 'sixty (60) days' for payment directly contradicts the termination clause which allows termination for 'the Distributor's failure to make full and prompt payment to the Company of all sums due and owing to it'. Changing the payment timeline impacts the definition of 'prompt payment', thus creating a direct conflict. The original text established 30 days as the timeline for prompt payment. The modified text extends it to 60, meaning the original termination clause is no longer aligned.

perturbation.original_text: In order to maintain the exclusive rights to sell, lease, distribute and service Products in the Market, the Distributor must use all commercially reasonably efforts to purchase for sale to subdistributors the following minimum quantities of the Products from the Company:
perturbation.changed_text: In order to maintain the exclusive rights to market, lease, distribute and service Products in the Territory, the Distributor must use all commercially reasonably efforts to acquire for sale to subdistributors the following minimum quantities of the Products from the Company:
perturbation.explanation: Here the terms 'sell' and 'Market' have been replaced with 'market' and 'Territory'. This introduces a contradiction because the rest of the document consistently refers to 'sell' and defines the geographical area as 'Market'. 'Market' is a defined term (Section C in Recitals), referring specifically to the State of Illinois. Using 'Territory' makes the definition ambiguous and contradicts the established definition. The text mentions Territory which is neither previously mentioned or defined. Similarly 'purchase' has been re-written to 'acquire' which in text means a cost incurred or effort in buying which in the sense makes no sense in the sentence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company hereby appoints Distributor as Company's exclusive distributor within the Market and grants to Distributor the exclusive right to sell and distribute Products within the Market, and Distributor hereby accepts such appointment and such grant, in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement. Distributor acknowledges that customers of other distributors of the Products may have sites, locations or operations in the Market, which will use the Products. Distributor will sell any and all Products required by such customers in the Market to those customers. Distributor also acknowledges that if its customers have sites, locations or operations outside the Market, in the market of another exclusive distributor of the Products, those customers will be required to purchase products from the applicable exclusive distributor in that market; otherwise, Distributor shall be free to sell to its customers in any market which does not have another exclusive distributor.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Market' to 'Territory' introduces a contradiction because the contradicted text explicitly defines and uses 'Market' as the geographical area in which the Distributor has exclusive rights. The introduction of 'Territory,' which is undefined, creates ambiguity and directly conflicts with the established and consistently used 'Market' term. Additionally, changing 'purchase' to 'acquire' disrupts the clarity of the obligation, as 'purchase' implies a direct transaction, while 'acquire' is broader and less precise in the context of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: The Company appoints the Distributor as an exclusive distributor of Products in the Market, subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company engages the Distributor as a preferred vendor of Products in the Market, subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The core relationship definition is altered, changing 'exclusive distributor' to 'preferred vendor'. This creates uncertainty regarding the extent of exclusivity and the Distributor's rights. The original text provided an exclusive right. This alteration creates uncertainty regarding whether the distributor can be replaced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company hereby appoints Distributor as Company's exclusive distributor within the Market and grants to Distributor the exclusive right to sell and distribute Products within the Market, and Distributor hereby accepts such appointment and such grant, in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement. Distributor acknowledges that customers of other distributors of the Products may have sites, locations or operations in the Market, which will use the Products. Distributor will sell any and all Products required by such customers in the Market to those customers. Distributor also acknowledges that if its customers have sites, locations or operations outside the Market, in the market of another exclusive distributor of the Products, those customers will be required to purchase products from the applicable exclusive distributor in that market; otherwise, Distributor shall be free to sell to its customers in any market which does not have another exclusive distributor.
perturbation.justification: The original text designated the Distributor as an 'exclusive distributor', while the changed text alters this to 'preferred vendor'. The contradicted text explicitly grants the Distributor the 'exclusive right' to sell and distribute Products within the Market. Changing 'exclusive distributor' to 'preferred vendor' directly contradicts this exclusive right, as a preferred vendor status does not inherently imply exclusivity, creating a strong inconsistency and uncertainty regarding the Distributor's rights and potentially allowing the Company to appoint other vendors.
